steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Martinez and colleagues concluded that H. ochraceiceps links T. caesius alarm calls specifically to danger. To support that conclusion, we need evidence that rules out the rival explanation: that the birds react to any sound, not just alarm calls.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "When Martínez and colleagues played control sounds of random noise in the vicinity of *H. ochraceiceps*, the birds displayed no reaction.": Shows the birds do NOT react to arbitrary sound — only to the alarm call. This isolates the alarm call as the meaningful trigger and directly supports the danger-association conclusion. ✓
  • "Bird species other than *H. ochraceiceps* also showed a tendency to freeze in place or scatter into vegetation when Martínez and colleagues played *T. caesius* alarm calls.": About other species, not about whether H. ochraceiceps specifically links the call to danger. Irrelevant. ✗
  • "Martínez and colleagues played alarm calls from different *T. caesius* individuals and observed no significant variation in the responses of *H. ochraceiceps*.": Shows consistency across individuals, but doesn't address whether the birds tie the calls to danger versus reacting to any sound. ✗
  • "In some instances, *H. ochraceiceps* froze in place or scattered into vegetation when Martínez and colleagues approached but before they began playing sounds.": Undermines the conclusion — the birds reacted without any alarm call at all, suggesting something other than the call triggered the response. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
